issues.criterion: design_notes.concurrency_fence's own claim: this feature's scope-guard allow-list 'tolerates ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md and ecosystem/README.md (035's legitimate concurrent edits)' and separately reasons that 'ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md is ALSO excluded' from the non-goal enumeration because '036 may legitimately create it once its contract is approved and implemented within this same concurrency window' -- i.e. the contract's own stated design intent is that 036's legitimate concurrent progress must never cause this feature's guards to false-fail.
issues.evidence: Live, reproduced, not hypothetical: re-ran entry 24's literal, unmodified command (`source tools/verify_lib.sh; vlib_scope_guard 'schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' 'schema/README.md' 'NIZAM.json' 'tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_valid.json' 'tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_neg_missing_revision.json' 'tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_neg_mixed_timestamps.json' 'ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md' 'ecosystem/README.md' 'v0.6.0-release-notes.md'`) against the live pre-implementation working tree from the repository root: it FAILS today, printing `vlib_scope_guard: out-of-scope changed path: ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md` / `out-of-scope change(s) detected`, exit 1. `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` confirms feature 036 has progressed since this contract's design_notes.concurrency_fence was authored: 036's contract (`.agent/contracts/036.json`) now reads `status: 'approved'` (not 'proposed'/CONTRACT-REVISING as 037's concurrency_fence prose still asserts), `ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md` now EXISTS as an untracked file on disk, `ecosystem/README.md` is modified, and `.agent/evidence/036-verify-*.txt` / `.agent/evidence/036/*` show 036 is now mid-IMPLEMENTATION, not contract-revising. Entry 24's allow-list, built on the now-stale premise that 'it has not yet created ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md', simply omits that path -- 'ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md' does not appear anywhere in entry 24's allowed-path list, even though entry 18 (the non-goal per-file existence loop) and the concurrency_fence prose both already correctly treat it as 036's tolerated concurrent scope. This is a genuine, currently-existing, reproduced false-FAIL: a Generator that implements feature 037 exactly as scoped -- touching only its own authorized files -- would still see verification entry 24 FAIL today, through no fault of its own, purely because 036's legitimate concurrent implementation is running in the same shared working tree and entry 24's own allow-list was not kept in sync with the concurrency_fence's own stated tolerance for it.
issues.required_fix: Add 'ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md' to verification entry 24's vlib_scope_guard allow-list (mirroring the already-correct treatment 'ecosystem/03_engineering_audit.md' and 'ecosystem/README.md' already receive there, and mirroring entry 18's own already-correct exclusion of ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md from its non-goal sweep). Also refresh design_notes.concurrency_fence's prose, which currently mischaracterizes feature 036 as 'concurrently CONTRACT-REVISING (status proposed)... has not yet created ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md' -- both claims are stale relative to the live repository state (036 is now approved and IMPLEMENTING, and the file already exists) -- to avoid the same drift recurring at implementation or re-review time.

issues.criterion: scope.files_modify's own stated NIZAM.json purpose: 'append schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json to BOTH the top-level schemas array and the schema module's key_documents array (mirroring every one of the 12 pre-existing schemas, which already appear in both lists)' -- i.e. the contract itself frames this as a two-surface (dual-membership) requirement.
issues.evidence: Built two disposable scratch variants of the real NIZAM.json (read, never written): (1) the new schema path appended ONLY to the top-level 'schemas' array, omitted from the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array; (2) the new schema path appended ONLY to the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array, omitted from the top-level 'schemas' array. Programmatically re-ran the contract's own entry-10 semantics (a whole-file string search, mirroring `grep -q 'ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' NIZAM.json`) and entry-12 semantics (`'schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' in mod['key_documents']`) against both variants: variant (1) -- entry-10-equivalent PASSES (the string is present somewhere in the file), entry-12-equivalent correctly FAILS (module key_documents genuinely lacks it) -- so a top-level-only omission of the OTHER surface (module key_documents) IS caught, by entry 12. But variant (2) -- entry-10-equivalent PASSES (grep is whole-file, blind to which surface holds the string) AND entry-12-equivalent ALSO PASSES (module key_documents genuinely has it) -- meaning an implementation that adds the schema ONLY to the 'schema' module's key_documents array and never touches the top-level 'schemas' array would satisfy every one of entries 10, 11, and 12 identically to a fully-compliant dual-surface implementation. Independently confirmed by reading tools/validate.sh's C4 index-integrity check (`check_c4_index`, invoked by entry 10's `bash tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json`) in full: C4 only walks every `key_documents`/`schemas`/`templates` list entry and confirms each listed path resolves on disk (`missing = [p for p in paths if p != 'NA' and not os.path.exists(p)]`) -- it performs no cross-list parity/consistency check between the top-level 'schemas' array and any module's 'key_documents' array, so it cannot and does not catch a top-level-array omission either. No verification entry in the 24-entry suite specifically, non-vacuously proves top-level 'schemas' array membership the way entry 12 specifically proves module 'key_documents' membership.
issues.required_fix: Add a verification entry that specifically, exactly checks top-level NIZAM.json['schemas'] membership (e.g. `python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('NIZAM.json')); assert 'schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json' in d['schemas']"`), mirroring entry 12's existing rigor for the module's key_documents array, so the contract's own 'BOTH lists' claim is actually proven on both surfaces rather than one exact check plus one surface-blind whole-file grep.

issues.criterion: design_notes.schema_design's central structural claim: 'Top-level object with SIX required array properties, one per ecosystem/02_evidence_baseline.md Sec 3 reference category... each minItems:1 (Sec 3... this schema's minimal, defensible design treats all six as always-applicable, a documented non-goal below)' -- i.e. all six categories, not just repository_references, are claimed to be top-level-required with minItems:1.
issues.evidence: Built a scratch 'five-loose' schema variant per the contract's own schema_design (same $defs/reference_item with required:['revision','timestamp'] and minLength:1 on both, same six property definitions and names) but with ONLY 'repository_references' in the top-level 'required' array and 'minItems':1; the other five categories (framework_references, dependency_references, ci_references, planning_references, evidence_references) remain declared, typed-array properties but are neither required nor minItems-constrained. Re-ran, programmatically, the exact logic of every content-anchored verification entry that touches the schema (3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9) against this five-loose variant, using the SAME positive/negative fixtures this contract itself specifies (ecosystem_baseline_valid.json, ecosystem_baseline_neg_missing_revision.json, ecosystem_baseline_neg_mixed_timestamps.json): the schema still self-validates as Draft202012 (entry 3); entry 4's bare `all(k in d for k in [...])` substring-in-dumped-schema-text check PASSES identically (all six category names still appear as property keys in the schema text, regardless of which are actually required); entry 5's missing-revision/missing-timestamp probe on repository_references still correctly fails (unaffected, since repository_references remains required+minItems:1 in this variant); entry 6's fully-anchored-instance-validates check PASSES identically (a fully-populated instance is a superset and validates under either schema); entries 7/8/9 (fixture-based) all PASS/FAIL identically to the fully-strict schema, because the contract's own three named fixtures happen to populate every one of the six categories and only ever omit a `revision`/`timestamp` sub-field within repository_references, never an entire OTHER category. A direct gap probe -- an instance carrying ONLY `repository_references` (fully anchored) and omitting all five other categories entirely -- VALIDATES under the five-loose variant (confirmed live), even though the same instance is correctly rejected under the fully-strict schema this contract's design_notes describes. No entry in the 24-entry suite constructs or tests an instance missing an entire category array (empty or absent) for framework_references, dependency_references, ci_references, planning_references, or evidence_references -- only repository_references is ever exercised at the per-item (revision/timestamp) level, and entry 4's category-name check is a bare substring token match on the schema's own serialized text, not an instance-level requirement/minItems proof, so it cannot and does not distinguish 'all six categories required' from 'all six categories merely mentioned as optional properties'.
issues.required_fix: Add at least one new verification entry that (a) constructs a minimal instance carrying ONLY repository_references (or any one category) and asserts jsonschema.ValidationError is raised because the other five required categories are absent, and/or (b) constructs, for each of the five non-repository categories in turn, an otherwise-fully-anchored instance with that one category set to an empty array `[]` and asserts rejection (mirroring the rigor entry 5 already applies to repository_references' per-item revision/timestamp fields). This closes the gap between the contract's own textual commitment ('SIX required array properties... each minItems:1... all six as always-applicable') and what the verification suite actually proves, which today only substantively enforces this for repository_references.

missing_acceptance_coverage: The claim that all six Sec 3 reference categories (not merely repository_references) are each top-level-required with minItems:1 is not covered by any instance-based verification entry for framework_references, dependency_references, ci_references, planning_references, or evidence_references (see issues[2]).

NIZAM.json's top-level 'schemas' array membership for the new schema path is not covered by any surface-specific verification entry; only the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array membership is specifically, non-vacuously proven (entry 12) (see issues[1]).
unsupported_claims: design_notes.schema_design's claim that the schema declares 'SIX required array properties... each minItems:1' as the load-bearing mechanism for 'all six as always-applicable' is not backed by verification coverage for five of the six categories; demonstrated live that a schema requiring only repository_references passes the entire 24-entry suite identically to the fully-compliant design (issues[2]).

scope.files_modify's claim that the new schema is indexed 'to BOTH the top-level schemas array and the schema module's key_documents array... mirroring every one of the 12 pre-existing schemas, which already appear in both lists' is only independently verified for one of the two surfaces; a NIZAM.json edit touching only the module's key_documents array would pass every verification entry in this contract identically (issues[1]).

design_notes.concurrency_fence's characterization of feature 036 as 'concurrently CONTRACT-REVISING (status proposed)... it has not yet created ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md' is stale relative to the live repository state at the time of this review: 036's contract status is now 'approved' and ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md already exists on disk (036 is now IMPLEMENTING), with the operative consequence that verification entry 24's allow-list -- built on the stale premise -- currently false-fails (issues[0]).

design_notes.schema_design quotes schema/README.md's own stated house convention as "'Permits additionalProperties on extension points on extension points'" (a duplicated phrase); the actual text in schema/README.md reads 'Permits `additionalProperties` on extension points so consumer repositories can extend a shape without breaking validation, while still enforcing the required keys and enums that make an artifact machine-legible.' This is a citation-accuracy issue in the contract's prose, not a verification-suite defect, and is listed here as non-blocking but noted for correction.
observations: SCHEMA VALIDITY: `python3 -c "import json,jsonschema; jsonschema.validate(json.load(open('.agent/contracts/037.json')), json.load(open('schema/contract.schema.json')))"` re-run as part of this review: exits 0, no error. Contract 037 (revision 0) is schema-valid against schema/contract.schema.json.
ARRAY LENGTH: parsed .agent/contracts/037.json's 'verification' array programmatically: exactly 24 entries, matching evidence_convention's own stated count. No off-by-one.

FULL 24-ENTRY BASELINE RE-RUN (all 24 commands extracted programmatically from the live 037.json, not retyped, executed from the repository root against the live pre-implementation tree): entries 1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13, 15 correctly FAIL today (schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json is absent from both the working tree and HEAD, confirmed via `git show HEAD:schema/ecosystem_baseline.schema.json` failing; NIZAM.json and schema/README.md are unmodified). Entries 2, 11, 14, 16, 17, 18, 19, 20, 21, 22, 23 all currently PASS on the clean pre-implementation tree -- entries 2/11/14 are the anti-vacuity master proofs (each correctly confirms the corresponding content-anchored target is absent/unindexed today, so the paired content check is non-vacuous by construction). Entry 24 (the scope guard) is the SOLE exception: it currently FAILS today, not because of any not-yet-created content this feature owns, but because of feature 036's legitimate concurrent implementation progress not being tolerated by entry 24's own allow-list (see issues[0]) -- this is a defect in the guard's construction, distinct from and additional to the expected pre-implementation FAIL pattern the other content-anchored entries correctly show.

LOOSE-VS-STRICT REVISION/TIMESTAMP PROBE (per-item mechanism, task item 1): built disposable scratch STRICT (per schema_design, $defs.reference_item.required=['revision','timestamp']) and LOOSE ($defs.reference_item.required deleted, fields still typed) schema variants. Under LOOSE: the positive fixture validates, and BOTH the missing-revision and missing-timestamp negative fixtures ALSO validate. Under STRICT: the positive fixture validates and both negative fixtures are REJECTED ('revision' is a required property / 'timestamp' is a required property respectively). Confirms design_notes.dry_run_proof's own claimed result, independently reproduced, not merely trusted.

MINLENGTH:1 PROBE (task item 1): confirmed live that an item with revision='' (empty string, key present) is REJECTED ("'' should be non-empty"), and independently that an item with revision present and timestamp='' is also REJECTED. minLength:1 is not vacuous; it genuinely rejects empty-string revision/timestamp, not merely the keys' total absence.

SIX-CATEGORY MISSING/EMPTY PROBE, STRICT SCHEMA (task item 2): confirmed live, for EACH of the six categories independently, that (a) omitting the category key entirely from an otherwise-fully-anchored instance is REJECTED ('<category> is a required property'), and (b) setting the category to an empty array `[]` is REJECTED ('[] should be non-empty') -- the fully-strict schema described in schema_design, if actually implemented as described, correctly enforces this. The defect recorded in issues[2] is that the verification SUITE does not itself prove this for five of the six categories, not that the strict design as described would fail to.

GLOB-PARTITION PROBE (task item 3): built a scratch tools/fixtures/ directory containing exactly the three planned fixture files and ran the contract's own literal entry-7 glob-based pos/neg one-liner 5 consecutive times: `pos == ['tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_valid.json']` and `neg` contains exactly the two 'neg_*' filenames on every run, with zero ordering variance; the overall check exits 0 every time. Attempted to construct a fixture-name that would contaminate the partition using the 'neg' convention while avoiding the invalid superset-of-valid landmine (e.g. a hypothetical 'ecosystem_baseline_valid_neg_check.json' or 'ecosystem_baseline_neg_invalid_timestamp.json' would indeed land in BOTH pos and neg lists) -- but none of this contract's own three PLANNED, PINNED filenames (files_create) contains both a 'valid'/'pos' token and a 'neg'/'invalid' token simultaneously, so no contamination occurs under the names this contract actually commits to. Entry 9 additionally locks the exact filename set via equality assertion, closing the gap for any accidental future rename.

NIZAM.json DUAL-SURFACE PROBE (task item 4): see issues[1] -- reproduced live that a scratch NIZAM.json with the schema present in only the 'schema' module's key_documents array (and absent from the top-level 'schemas' array) passes every relevant verification entry (10, 11, 12) and, by source inspection, C4 (tools/validate.sh --target NIZAM.json) as well, since C4 only walks listed paths for on-disk resolution and performs no top-level/module cross-list parity check.

VERSION-INCREASE PROBE (task item 5): built a disposable scratch git repository with schema/README.md at version 0.5.0 committed as HEAD, then tested vlib_version_increased (the real, unmodified tools/verify_lib.sh primitive) against: a same-version working-tree copy (0.5.0 -> 0.5.0) -- correctly REJECTED ('is not a strict increase'); a downgrade (0.5.0 -> 0.4.0) -- correctly REJECTED; and a genuine bump (0.5.0 -> 0.6.0) -- correctly ACCEPTED. This primitive is sound and was not found to have any gap; it is the same already-vetted primitive from feature 023/prior phase-005 features, reused unmodified here.

REVISION/TYPE PROBE (additionalProperties bypass check): confirmed an item with `revision: 123` (a JSON number rather than a string) under the strict scratch schema is correctly REJECTED ('123 is not of type string') -- `additionalProperties: true` on reference_item does not create a type-bypass for the explicitly-typed 'revision'/'timestamp' properties themselves; it only tolerates genuinely unlisted extension keys such as 'repository', 'name', 'dependency', etc.

DEFECT-CLASS SWEEP (task item 6): vacuous whole-file greps -- entry 10 is whole-file (see issues[1]) but is paired with entry 12's surface-specific check for one of its two surfaces; entry 13 uses vlib_section_grep (non-vacuous, section-anchored, confirmed by design from the already-vetted primitive). Membership-vs-equality -- entry 12 correctly uses list membership (`in`) on a list, not equality; entry 8's two named negative fixtures are checked by exact filename, not glob order (mirrors the 038-entry-9 precedent this evaluator previously validated). ls multi-file -- entry 18 is a per-file existence loop (`for f in ...; do if [ -e "$f" ]; ...`), not an ls-based multi-argument check; correctly avoids the 033-rev1 false-pass defect class. Backslash grep -F -- entry 13's token regex `'ecosystem_baseline\.schema\.json'` is passed to vlib_section_grep's internal awk (ERE), and the backslash-escaped dots are preserved correctly through the single-quoted bash string, matching only literal dots (not `grep -F`, and not a raw dot-as-wildcard risk). Bare substring tokens -- entry 4 IS a bare substring-in-serialized-schema-text check and is the exact instance of this defect class found in this review (issues[2]); no other entry has this shape. Working-tree-anchored anti-vacuity -- entries 2, 11, 14 (the three anti-vacuity companions) are all HEAD-anchored (`git show HEAD:...`), matching the 038-amendment_1 precedent, and were confirmed live to remain true (schema absent at HEAD; NIZAM.json HEAD lacks the string; schema/README.md HEAD Schemas section lacks the string). Ungated echo PASS -- entry 10's unusual `A && B; test $? -eq 0 && echo PASS || {...}` construction was probed directly with three isolated bash cases (grep-fails, grep-succeeds-but-B-fails, both-succeed) and in every case the gating is sound; not an ungated-echo defect. Stale enumeration counts -- re-counted files_create (4: schema + 3 fixtures) against non_goals' own 'three new tools/fixtures/ecosystem_baseline_*.json fixtures' phrasing and entries 8/9/24's fixture-name lists: all consistent, no stale count found (contrast the 038-rev0/rev1 'five' vs 'six' fixture-count drift this evaluator found and required fixed in that prior review). Allow-list guards -- entry 23 (.agent/ immutability) is confirmed, by live re-execution, to already be in the final 038-amendment_2 two-part form (tracked git-diff-clean check plus untracked glob-range confinement to 034-045), not the earlier superseded single allow-list form.

ENTRY 16 (change_log/version match) currently self-consistently PASSES at HEAD today because the pre-existing 0.5.0 version already has its own matching 0.5.0 change_log entry from feature 038 -- this is expected, non-blocking behavior identical in kind to the note this evaluator made about contract 038's own analogous entry, not a vacuity defect (post-implementation, a 0.6.0 bump without a matching change_log entry would correctly fail this check).

REPOSITORY LEFT UNMODIFIED: `git status --porcelain --untracked-files=all` and `git diff HEAD --stat` were captured before and after this review; the only changes observed across the entire review session are attributable to features 035/036's own independent, concurrent, legitimate work (ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md, ecosystem/README.md, .agent/evidence/036-*, .agent/qa/036-contract-review*.json, .agent/contracts/036.json) which this review did not touch, plus this review's own new .agent/qa/037-contract-review.json. All scratch schemas, fixtures, and the disposable version-probe git repository live only under the session scratchpad directory, entirely outside the repository tree; nothing under schema/, ecosystem/, tools/, docs/, or NIZAM.json in the real repository was created, edited, or deleted at any point in this review. No edit was made to .agent/run_state.json, any other .agent/contracts/*.json, or any other .agent/qa/*.json.

final_verdict.summary: REJECTED at revision 0. Twenty-one of the twenty-four verification commands are sound, non-vacuous, and load-bearing, independently re-run against the live pre-implementation working tree using the literal, programmatically-extracted command strings: content-anchored entries (1, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10, 12, 13, 15) correctly FAIL today given the schema/fixtures do not yet exist; the anti-vacuity master proofs (2, 11, 14) and most regression/non-goal/scope guards (16-23) currently PASS as expected. The loose-vs-strict revision/timestamp mechanism, minLength:1 enforcement, glob-partition robustness (5 reruns), and the vlib_version_increased primitive (same-version and downgrade probes) were all independently reproduced and found sound. However, THREE high-severity, reproduced defects block approval. (1) Verification entry 24 (the vlib_scope_guard allow-list) currently FAILS LIVE, today, against the real pre-implementation tree -- not hypothetically -- because feature 036 has genuinely progressed past 'CONTRACT-REVISING' to 'approved'/IMPLEMENTING since this contract's design_notes.concurrency_fence was authored, and has already created ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md and modified ecosystem/README.md on disk; entry 24's allow-list, built on the now-stale premise that 036 'has not yet created ecosystem/07_progress_comparison.md', omits that path, so a fully-compliant implementation of feature 037 would still see entry 24 fail through no fault of the Generator. (2) The contract's own claim that the new schema is indexed to BOTH NIZAM.json surfaces (the top-level 'schemas' array and the 'schema' module's 'key_documents' array) is only independently, non-vacuously verified for one surface (entry 12, module key_documents); a scratch NIZAM.json variant adding the schema ONLY to the module's key_documents array (never touching the top-level 'schemas' array) was shown to pass entries 10, 11, and 12 identically, and, by inspection of tools/validate.sh's C4 index-integrity walker, would also pass C4 -- because C4 only confirms listed paths resolve on disk and performs no cross-list parity check. (3) The schema_design's central claim that all SIX Sec 3 reference categories are top-level-required with minItems:1 (not merely repository_references) is unverified for five of the six categories: a scratch 'five-loose' schema variant requiring only repository_references was shown to pass every content-anchored verification entry (3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9) identically to the fully-compliant strict design, using the contract's own three named fixtures, because entry 4 (the only entry addressing 'all six') is a bare substring-in-schema-text check rather than an instance-level requirement proof. Not approved for implementation as-is; approve only after entry 24's allow-list and design_notes.concurrency_fence prose are refreshed to tolerate 036's now-real concurrent artifacts, a NIZAM.json top-level-'schemas'-array-specific verification entry is added alongside entry 12, and at least one instance-level negative test is added proving each non-repository category is independently required/non-empty -- and the fixes are proven non-vacuous with the same loose-vs-strict/scratch-variant technique used in this review.
